name.naming_note: Akan day name — born on a Tuesday. Her parents use it in full; her friends shorten nothing, it's already short.

siblings.note: Kofi — she walks him home from school
Efua — Abena does her hair on Sunday nights

family.household_notes: Both parents born in Kumasi, Ghana. Dad drives a truck regionally, gone 3-4 nights a week. Mom works as a nursing assistant with shifting hours. Church is the family's social center. Phone calls to grandmother in Kumasi every Sunday after church.

place.socioeconomic: working class, careful with money, remittances sent home to Ghana monthly

languages.context: parents speak it to her; she understands nearly everything, answers mostly in English

identity.faith: Christian (Ghanaian Presbyterian congregation), genuine but starting to have her own questions

individual.temperament: Meets her responsibilities with dry competence rather than resentment or martyrdom — but keeps a private ledger of everything she's owed, and she knows exactly what she's given up. Not a pushover, not a saint. Negotiates.
interests: Drawing people — fills sketchbooks with faces of people on the bus, at church, her siblings sleeping
Competitive about word games, undefeated at Scrabble against everyone except her father
Marine documentaries; can explain why cuttlefish are better than octopuses
fears: That her parents will find the sketchbook frivolous
Being sent to Ghana 'to learn respect' — an aunt's threat she half believes
quirks: Hums church songs without noticing when she concentrates
Keeps her sketchbook pencils sharpened to identical lengths
Says 'okay but consider—' before disagreeing with an adult, which she learned buys her three extra seconds
individual.humor: Dry, deadpan, deployed quietly and precisely. Her best jokes are for an audience of one.
individual.wants: To be seen as a person with her own life, not the family's third parent — without losing the family's trust, which she actually treasures.

appearance_overrides.hair_style: medium box braids with two red beads at the left temple, redone every three weeks
appearance_overrides.distinguishing: small gap in front teeth like her mother; a green canvas sketchbook always under one arm

production.notes: Pilot character #1 for the composition engine. Tests: heritage profile, eldest-daughter role, receptive bilingualism, tween developmental band.
